What is `std::unique_ptr`? #q15 #smart-p: Show `std::unique_ptr` usage. Create an `int` with `std::make_unique<int>(5)`; read its value and let automatic cleanup run at scope exit — copy assignment should fail to compile. #q15 #smart-pointers #memory-pointers
How do C++20 concepts relate to template: Show C++20 concepts constraining templates. Define `Addable` requiring `a + b` convertible to `T`, then implement `add` only for types satisfying that concept. #q31 #templates #templates-generic
How does template argument deduction wor: Show template argument deduction from call sites. Call `myFunc(5)` to deduce `T=int` and `myFunc<double>(5)` to force `T=double`. #q40 #template-argument-deduction #templates-generic
What is class template argument deductio: Show class template argument deduction (CTAD). Construct `std::pair p(1, 2.5)` without explicit template args; constrain a deduction site with a C++20 `std::integral` concept on `integerFunc`. #q40 #template-argument-deduction #templates-generic
How do C++20 concepts compare to SFINAE?: Show C++20 concepts replacing SFINAE. Split `print_number` into `std::integral T` and `std::floating_point T` overloads with the same call-site behavior as trait-based dispatch. #q37 #sfinae #templates-generic
What is tag dispatching with SFINAE? #q3: Show tag dispatching with SFINAE. Route `process(T)` to different `process_impl` helpers depending on whether `T` is integral. #q37 #sfinae #templates-generic
What is the syntax for variadic template: Show variadic template syntax. Declare `myFunction` taking a parameter pack `Ts... args` from template parameter pack `typename... Ts`. #q35 #variadic-templates #templates-generic
How is perfect forwarding used with vari: Show perfect forwarding with variadic templates. Write `wrapper(Args&&... args)` that forwards every argument to `someFunction` via `std::forward`. #q35 #variadic-templates #templates-generic
How do you solve the diamond problem in: Show solving the diamond problem with virtual inheritance. Give `Derived` an initializer list that constructs the shared `Base` once alongside `BaseA` and `BaseB`. #q26 #diamond-problem #oop-concepts
How do you prevent unwanted function hid: Show preventing unwanted function hiding. Give `Derived` its own no-arg `display()` replacement and a `print(double)` overload while keeping `Base` `print` overloads callable. #q27 #function-hiding #oop-concepts
What are inheritance access specifiers (: Show inheritance access specifier effects. Given `Base` with `public x`, `protected y`, and private `z`, show which members `PublicDerived` exposes after `public` inheritance. #q21 #inheritance #oop-concepts
What is the syntax for `try`, `catch`, a: Show the syntax for `try`, `catch`, and `throw`. Write a `try` block, a `catch (ExceptionType& e)` handler, and a `throw ExceptionObject()` statement. #q42 #try-catch-throw #exceptions
What are the forms of `noexcept`? #q43 #: Show the forms of `noexcept`. Declare `func1()` unconditionally non-throwing and `func2()` conditionally `noexcept` when `sizeof(int) == 4`. #q43 #noexcept #exceptions
What happens with uncaught exceptions an: Show uncaught exceptions violating `noexcept`. Define `func() noexcept` that throws `std::runtime_error`; note immediate termination instead of normal unwinding. #q45 #uncaught-exceptions #exceptions
How do you use `std::variant` and `std::: Show `std::variant` and `std::visit`. Store `int` or `std::string` in a variant; use `std::visit` with a lambda to print the active member. #q88 #variant-visit #advanced-cpp
How do you access values in a `std::vari: Show accessing `std::variant` members. Read with `std::get<T>` when the type is known; use `get_if` or `holds_alternative` when it is not. #q88 #variant-visit #advanced-cpp
Explain the concept of concepts in C++20: Show defining a C++20 concept. Write `template<typename T> concept Integral = std::is_integral_v<T>;` and use it to constrain a template. #q86 #concepts #advanced-cpp
How do you define and combine concepts?: Show combining concepts. Build `SignedIntegral` with `std::integral<T> && std::signed_integral<T>` and use it in a constrained template. #q86 #concepts #advanced-cpp
How do you implement a singleton pattern: Show Meyers singleton. Return a function-local `static Singleton&` from `getInstance()`; delete copy/move so only one instance exists. #q84 #singleton #advanced-cpp
What are empty pack and binary fold rule: Show empty and binary fold rules. Evaluate `(args + ...)` over a pack; know when `+` needs a default value for an empty pack. #q83 #fold-expressions #advanced-cpp
What are common use cases for `std::opti: Show `std::optional` for missing values. Return `std::nullopt` when a parse fails; use `value_or` or `if (opt)` before dereferencing. #q85 #std-optional #advanced-cpp
What are module partitions and global mo: Show module partitions. Declare `export module M:Part;` in a partition interface and import it from the primary module interface. #q90 #modules #advanced-cpp
How do you create and manage threads usi: Show creating and joining `std::thread`. Start a worker thread, pass arguments by value or reference with `ref`, and `join()` before `main` ends. #q62 #std-thread #concurrency
How do you synchronize shared data with: Show synchronizing shared data with threads. Protect a shared counter with `std::mutex` and `lock_guard` while multiple threads increment. #q62 #std-thread #concurrency
How do you use condition variables for t: Show condition-variable synchronization. Wait on `std::condition_variable` with a predicate until shared data is ready; notify from the producer thread. #q65 #condition-variable #concurrency
What is the worker loop pattern in a thr: Show a thread-pool worker loop. Workers wait on a task queue, pop callable tasks under a mutex, execute, and loop until shutdown. #q68 #thread-pool #concurrency
How does `enqueue` return results from p: Show `enqueue` returning futures. Wrap submitted work in `std::packaged_task` and return `std::future` to the caller for the result. #q68 #thread-pool #concurrency
What advanced techniques address race co: Show advanced race-condition mitigation. Combine `std::atomic` counters with `mutex`-protected non-atomic state updated together. #q69 #race-conditions #concurrency
What is sequential consistency (`seq_cst: Show `memory_order_seq_cst`. Use the default ordering on `atomic` loads/stores when you need a single total order across threads. #q70 #memory-ordering #concurrency
What are lambda expressions in C++? #q71: Show a basic lambda. Capture by value or reference and use the lambda as a predicate with `std::count_if` on a `vector<int>`. #q71 #lambdas #cpp11-beyond
Explain the `auto` keyword and type infe: Show practical `auto`. Deduce `vector<int>`, iterator types, and range-for variables; use `auto*` when you need a raw pointer type. #q72 #auto #cpp11-beyond
How does uniform initialization prevent: Show narrowing checks with `{}`. `int x{3.14}` should fail to compile; braced lists reject narrowing conversions. #q73 #uniform-init #cpp11-beyond
How does initializer_list constructor pr: Show `initializer_list` priority. Prefer the `vector(initializer_list<T>)` constructor over element-wise construction when passing `{1,2,3}`. #q73 #uniform-init #cpp11-beyond
What are delegating constructors? #q75 #: Show delegating constructors on `Person`. Chain `Person()` to `Person("Unknown", 0)` in the constructor initializer list. #q75 #delegating-ctors #cpp11-beyond
How does a range-based for loop work? #q: Show range-based for loops. Iterate a `vector`, a C array, and a `map` with `for (auto x : container)` syntax. #q74 #range-for #cpp11-beyond
When should you use reference vs value i: Show range-for reference vs value. Use `const auto&` to read without copying large elements; use `auto&` to mutate in place. #q74 #range-for #cpp11-beyond
What C++17/C++20 enhancements apply to r: Show structured bindings in range-for. Iterate `map` entries as `for (const auto& [key, value] : m)` (C++17). #q74 #range-for #cpp11-beyond
What are key use cases for rvalue refere: Show rvalue reference use cases. Bind to temporaries, implement move construction, and transfer `unique_ptr` ownership with `std::move`. #q76 #rvalue-refs #cpp11-beyond
What are durations and time points in `s: Show `chrono` durations and time points. Measure elapsed time with `high_resolution_clock` and sleep with `sleep_for(milliseconds)`. #q78 #std-chrono #cpp11-beyond
What is loop unrolling and when is it be: Show manual loop unrolling. Sum an array four elements per iteration to reduce branch and induction-variable overhead. #q94 #loop-unrolling #performance
How do you avoid false sharing? #q97 #fa: Show avoiding false sharing. Pad `atomic` counters to separate cache lines so threads do not invalidate each other's lines. #q97 #false-sharing #performance
What tools reduce dynamic memory overhea: Show reducing dynamic memory overhead. Prefer `vector` over manual `new[]`, and use `reserve` before bulk inserts. #q92 #memory-optimization #performance
What header and template practices speed: Show compile-time reduction practices. Use forward declarations, include what you use, and extern template declarations where appropriate. #q99 #compile-times #performance
What core mechanisms power lock-free cod: Show lock-free coordination. Use `compare_exchange_weak` in a retry loop to update a shared atomic head pointer. #q100 #lock-free #performance
What move operation signatures and when: Show move operation signatures. Declare move ctor/assignment taking `T&&`; call `std::move` when transferring ownership from a named source. #q20 #move-semantics #memory-pointers
